# Clipman

> Wayland-native clipboard history manager for Linux, built with GTK 4 and libadwaita. Press Super+V to search, pin and paste from a local SQLite-backed history. No telemetry; sensitive data auto-clears.

- [Project page](https://mohammedel-sayedahmed.github.io/clipman/)
- [Source](https://github.com/MohammedEl-sayedAhmed/clipman)
- [Design workspace](https://mohammedel-sayedahmed.github.io/clipman/design/index.html)
- [Changelog](https://github.com/MohammedEl-sayedAhmed/clipman/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)

## Overview

Clipman is a clipboard history manager built specifically for modern Linux desktops running Wayland. Unlike X11-era tools that poll the clipboard or rely on root-window hacks, Clipman cooperates with the compositor through documented protocols: a GNOME Shell extension talking over D-Bus on GNOME sessions, and `wl-clipboard` (`wl-paste --watch`) as a fallback on KDE Plasma, Sway, Hyprland, and other wlroots-based compositors. The daemon runs as a user systemd service and is stateless beyond a single SQLite database under `~/.local/share/clipman/`.

## Architecture

Clipman is written in Python 3.10–3.12 and built on GTK 4 + libadwaita 1.4+. The repository ships two halves: a long-lived daemon (the `clipman/` Python package, entry point `clipman.py`) that watches clipboard selections, persists them in SQLite, and renders the popup UI on demand; and a GNOME Shell extension (`extension/extension.js`) that runs inside the compositor's gjs process and forwards `owner-changed` clipboard events to the daemon over D-Bus.

The daemon's UI layer is libadwaita-first:

- `clipman/window.py` builds an `Adw.ApplicationWindow` with an `Adw.HeaderBar` and an `Adw.ActionRow`-driven history list — the popup that opens on Super+V.
- `clipman/preferences.py` ships the settings surface as a standalone `Adw.PreferencesWindow` with **six panes**: Appearance (Catppuccin theme + accent + opacity + font size), Privacy (start-in-incognito + sensitive-data auto-clear + purge-now), Shortcuts (toggle binding + paste mode), Storage (max entries + database location + backup/restore), Updates (opt-out daily check, see ADR 0007), and About.
- `clipman/snippets_dialog.py` wraps the reusable-snippet editor in an `Adw.NavigationSplitView` master-detail dialog with a searchable list on the left and an editor form on the right (template variables `{{date}}`, `{{clipboard}}` included).
- `clipman/edge_states.py` declares **16 `StateSpec` entries** — empty history, no search results, incognito paused, sensitive auto-cleared, first-run, extension missing, backup failed, and the other intentional edge moments — dispatched at render time by `render_edge_state` into one of `Adw.StatusPage`, `Adw.Banner`, or `Adw.AlertDialog`.
- `clipman/style.css` overrides libadwaita's `@named-color` tokens (`@accent_color`, `@window_bg_color`, `@card_bg_color`, …) with the Catppuccin Mocha (dark) and Catppuccin Latte (light) palettes, so every Adw widget picks up the theme automatically without per-widget CSS rules.
- `clipman/_version.py` is a leaf module holding the single `__version__` string literal; `clipman/__init__.py` re-exports it so the public `clipman.__version__` API still works while submodules can import the constant without re-entering the package root (CodeQL `py/cyclic-import`).

The daemon exposes a session-bus D-Bus interface at `com.clipman.Daemon` (`/com/clipman/Daemon`) for the toggle keybinding and clipboard ingest; the extension owns `org.gnome.Shell.Extensions.clipman` for paste-keystroke and window-placement calls back into the compositor. All storage is local: no network calls (except the opt-out daily update check against `api.github.com/repos/.../releases/latest`), no telemetry, no analytics.

## Supported compositors

- GNOME 45+ (Mutter / Wayland) via the bundled Shell extension
- KDE Plasma 6 (KWin / Wayland) via `wl-clipboard`
- Sway, Hyprland, river, Niri and other wlroots compositors via `wl-clipboard`
- X11 sessions are intentionally unsupported; legacy tools cover that case

## Privacy and security

Sensitive entries (passwords detected via heuristics, content marked secret by the source application, anything captured during an explicit incognito toggle) are auto-cleared after a configurable TTL (default 45 seconds). The SQLite file is created mode `0600`, the daemon refuses to start if permissions are loose, and the threat model is documented at `docs/threat-model.md`. Image clips are stored as content-addressed blobs under `~/.local/share/clipman/blobs/` and pruned with the parent entry.

## Features

- Full-text search across history with FTS5
- Pinning to keep entries past the history-size cap
- Reusable snippets with template variables (`{{date}}`, `{{clipboard}}`, `{{cursor}}`)
- Catppuccin Mocha and Latte themes; respects system dark/light preference
- 70 translatable strings in `po/clipman.pot`, community translations welcome

## Distribution

Released under Apache-2.0. Packaged for Snap Store, PyPI (`pipx install clipman-clipboard`), AUR (`clipman-clipboard`), and as standalone `.deb` / `.rpm` artifacts attached to each GitHub release.
